 {"id":"gt-ay1r","title":"gt molecule current: Show what agent should be working on","description":"Query what an agent identity is supposed to be working on via breadcrumb trail.

## Command
```bash
gt molecule current <identity>
gt molecule current gastown/furiosa
gt molecule current deacon
```

## Logic
1. Find handoff bead for identity (pinned bead titled "<role> Handoff")
2. Parse attachment field → molecule ID
3. If no attachment → "naked" (no active molecule)
4. If attached → load molecule, find current step:
   - bd ready --parent=<mol-id> → next unblocked step
   - Or first in_progress step

## Output
```
Identity: gastown/furiosa
Handoff:  gt-8v2 (Furiosa Handoff)
Molecule: gt-mol-abc (mol-polecat-work)
Progress: 3/8 steps complete
Current:  gt-mol-abc.4 - verify-tests
```

Or if naked:
```
Identity: gastown/angharad
Handoff:  gt-9x1 (Angharad Handoff)
Molecule: (none attached)
Status:   naked - awaiting work assignment
```

## Use Cases
- Mayor checking what polecats are doing
- Witness verifying polecat progress
- Debug: "why isnt this polecat working?"
- Deacon patrol: track all agent states","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-21T21:34:01.430109-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T23:43:41.533695-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T23:43:41.533695-08:00"}

-{"id":"gt-az41","title":"mol-ready-work variants: safe vs aggressive","description":"## Summary

Create two variants of mol-ready-work:

### mol-ready-work-safe (default)
- PRs: review and comment, but require human approval for merge
- Issues: triage only, don't auto-close
- Beads: implement but create PR instead of pushing to main

### mol-ready-work-aggressive  
- PRs: can approve and merge directly
- Issues: can close duplicates/invalid
- Beads: can push directly to main

## Implementation
Could be a single molecule with a `{{mode}}` variable, or two separate protos.

 {"id":"gt-b1g","title":"MVP Cutover: GGT replaces PGT for batch work","description":"When this is closed, stop using town and start using gt.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. gt spawn assigns issue to polecat and starts session
2. gt spawn --epic spawns workers for all epic children
3. gt session manages tmux lifecycle  
4. gt send / gt inbox work for mail
5. Refinery processes merge queue with semantic merges
6. Integration branches created and landed correctly
7. gt stop --all halts all sessions
8. One successful test batch completed end-to-end

## What Must Work

- Spawn polecat with issue assignment
- Spawn workers for epic children
- Session start/stop/attach
- Mail send/inbox/read
- Refinery merge loop (semantic)
- Integration branch → main landing
- Witness cleanup protocol
- Emergency stop

## What Can Be Deferred

- Doctor checks (use PGT)
- TUI dashboard
- Plugin system
- Federation
- Ephemeral rigs
- Detailed landing reports

## Test Plan

1. Create epic with 2 tasks, spawn 2 workers
2. Verify polecats get assigned and sessions start
3. Simulate task completion
4. Verify Refinery merges to integration
5. Verify landing to main
6. Verify cleanup

## Validation

Run one real batch implementing GGT issues using GGT.

## Note

No "swarm IDs" - just spawn workers for epic, let merge queue coordinate.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-16T00:11:09.148751-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T03:13:30.970314-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T03:13:30.970314-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-b1g","depends_on_id":"gt-u1j.19","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T00:11:36.196292-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-b1g","depends_on_id":"gt-kmn.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T00:11:36.273483-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-b1g","depends_on_id":"gt-kmn.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T00:11:36.351097-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-b1g","depends_on_id":"gt-kmn.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T00:11:36.431641-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-b1g","depends_on_id":"gt-u1j.22","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T00:11:36.511124-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-b1g","depends_on_id":"gt-ov2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T00:11:51.609649-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-b1g","depends_on_id":"gt-rm3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T00:11:51.69062-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-b1g","depends_on_id":"gt-u1j.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T21:36:32.942855-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-b1g","depends_on_id":"gt-u1j.12","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T21:36:35.053559-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-drbd","title":"Add no-PR instructions to mol-polecat-work at two points","description":"Update mol-polecat-work in builtin_molecules.go to explicitly forbid GitHub PRs.

## Two Points to Add Instructions

### 1. submit-work step
When polecat is ready to submit:
- Push branch to origin
- Create beads merge-request issue
- DO NOT use gh pr create or GitHub PRs

### 2. CLAUDE.md polecat context
Add to polecat role instructions:
- Never use gh pr create
- Never create GitHub pull requests
- The Refinery processes merges via beads MR issues

## Why Two Points
- Molecule step description guides the workflow
- CLAUDE.md reinforces at context level
- Belt and suspenders approach

## Implementation
1. Update PolecatWorkMolecule() submit-work step description
2. Update prompts/roles/polecat.md with explicit prohibition

Related: gt-44wh (general no-PR bug)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:44:51.497283-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T15:06:02.248711-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T15:06:02.248711-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-drbd","depends_on_id":"gt-44wh","type":"related","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:44:57.503314-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
-{"id":"gt-drp5","title":"mol-ready-work: graceful GitHub/label handling","description":"## Summary

mol-ready-work assumes:
- GitHub repo exists with gh CLI configured
- Labels 'untriaged' and 'triaged' exist

Should handle gracefully:
1. No GitHub repo (beads-only project) → skip GH backlogs
2. Missing labels → skip that backlog tier, don't error
3. gh CLI not authenticated → warn and skip GH backlogs

## Implementation
Add checks in scan-backlogs step:
```bash
# Check if gh is available and authenticated
if gh auth status &>/dev/null; then
  # scan GH backlogs
fi
```

## Parent

 {"id":"gt-ds3h3","title":"gt mol status: role detection fails from polecat directory","description":"When running 'gt mol status' from a polecat's worktree directory (e.g., ~/gt/gastown/polecats/furiosa), the role detection incorrectly returns 'mayor' instead of 'gastown/furiosa'.

## Root Cause
The detectRole() function in prime.go calculates relPath from townRoot (~gt) to cwd (~/gt/gastown/polecats/furiosa), giving 'gastown/polecats/furiosa'. It then checks parts[0] == 'mayor' but 'gastown' != 'mayor', so it falls through to rig detection where it treats 'gastown' as a rig name.

However, the code then looks for parts[1] == 'polecats' but the actual check is parts[1] == 'polecats' which should work...

## Actual Issue
Need to debug further - the hook file IS created correctly by spawn, but gt mol status can't find it because it's looking for the wrong agent identity.

## Expected
Running from ~/gt/gastown/polecats/furiosa should detect:
- Role: polecat
- Rig: gastown  
- Polecat: furiosa
- Agent identity: gastown/furiosa

## Actual
- Role: mayor
- Agent identity: mayor

This prevents polecats from seeing their slung work via gt mol status.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-25T01:55:54.680601-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T02:00:43.560153-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-25T02:00:43.560153-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-dt5","title":"Define Engineer as the Refinery agent role","description":"Clarify the distinction:

- **Refinery** = place/module/directory/workspace
  - `<rig>/refinery/` directory structure
  - `gt refinery start/stop/status` commands
  - tmux session name: `refinery` or `<rig>-refinery`

- **Engineer** = role/agent who works in the Refinery
  - CLAUDE.md prompting: "You are an Engineer..."
  - Documentation: "The Engineer processes merge requests..."
  - Mail address: `<rig>/engineer` (or `<rig>/refinery`?)

Updates needed:
- Add Engineer role description to docs
- Update CLAUDE.md templates for refinery agents
- Keep `gt refinery` commands as-is (they manage the place)
- Internal code stays `internal/refinery/` (the module)

Future consideration: Multiple Engineers in one Refinery for parallel merge processing.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T23:02:18.591842-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T21:00:51.09424-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-25T21:00:51.09424-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-dt5","depends_on_id":"gt-h5n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T23:02:55.577196-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-rm3","title":"CLI: gt refinery commands (start, stop, status, queue)","description":"CLI commands for managing the Refinery agent.

## Commands

```bash
gt refinery start <rig>      # Start refinery for a rig
gt refinery stop <rig>       # Stop refinery
gt refinery status <rig>     # Show refinery status
gt refinery queue <rig>      # Show merge queue
```

## gt refinery start

Starts the Refinery daemon for the specified rig.

Options:
- --foreground: Run in foreground (default: background)
- --auto-merge: Enable auto-merge (default: from config)

## gt refinery stop

Stops a running Refinery. Gracefully finishes current MR if processing.

## gt refinery status

Shows:
- Running state (running/stopped)
- Current MR being processed (if any)
- Queue length
- Last merge time
- Recent activity

## gt refinery queue

Shows the merge queue:
```
Merge queue for 'wyvern':
  1. [pending] Toast/polecat-auth-fix (15m ago)
  2. [pending] Capable/polecat-new-feature (5m ago)
  
1 merged today, 0 rejected
```

## Implementation

Uses gt-ov2 (Refinery agent) for daemon functionality.","status":"tombstone","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-15T23:22:24.754361-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T01:30:41.67682-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-rm3","depends_on_id":"gt-ov2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-15T23:22:30.679909-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}],"deleted_at":"2025-12-25T01:30:41.67682-08:00","deleted_by":"batch delete","delete_reason":"batch delete","original_type":"task"}
 {"id":"gt-ro5hc","title":"Digest: mol-deacon-patrol","description":"Patrol 20: all healthy","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-26T22:02:56.374874-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-26T22:02:56.374874-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-26T22:02:56.374826-08:00"}
-{"id":"gt-ronyn","title":"CRITICAL: gt doctor orphan-processes kills active crew sessions","description":"## Problem

The deacon's patrol runs `gt doctor --fix` which includes an orphan-processes check. This check is incorrectly identifying ACTIVE crew sessions as orphaned and killing them.

## Impact

- Crew workers are being killed mid-session
- Work is lost
- The deacon is currently disabled until this is fixed

## Root Cause

The orphan-processes detection logic in `internal/doctor/` doesn't properly identify crew workers. It likely:
1. Doesn't recognize the `gt-<rig>-<name>` session pattern for crew
2. Or misidentifies the Claude process as orphaned when it has a valid tmux parent

## Workaround

The patrol formula was updated to instruct deacon not to run `--fix` if orphan-processes reports any PIDs. But this workaround failed - the deacon still killed crew.

## Fix Required

1. Update `internal/doctor/identity_check.go` or `branch_check.go` to properly detect crew sessions
2. Add crew session patterns to the valid session detection
3. Add test coverage for crew worker scenarios

## Files

- internal/doctor/identity_check.go
- internal/doctor/branch_check.go  
- internal/cmd/doctor.go

## Related

- Commit 3452b041 attempted to fix via formula instructions but failed
- The digest hq-yedg claimed to file gt-vj5zc but never did","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-27T00:14:05.870126-08:00","created_by":"deacon","updated_at":"2025-12-27T00:14:05.870126-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-rp0k","title":"Extend auto-continue to polecats (not just crew)","description":"gt prime currently only outputs AUTO-CONTINUE MODE for crew workers.
Polecats with attached work should also auto-continue.

## Current behavior
outputCrewAttachmentStatus() in prime.go:
- Only runs for RoleCrew
- Outputs '→ AUTO-CONTINUE MODE' when attached work detected

## Desired behavior
- Rename to outputAttachmentStatus() or similar
- Run for RoleCrew AND RolePolecat
- Same directive: if attachment exists, work immediately

## The Propulsion Principle
'If you find something on your hook, YOU RUN IT.'

This applies to ALL workers, not just crew.

## Implementation
1. Extend role check in outputCrewAttachmentStatus()
2. Adjust assignee detection for polecats vs crew
3. Test with both worker types","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T16:43:22.149252-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T23:40:12.049577-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T23:40:12.049577-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-rr1i","title":"mol-swarm-cleanup: Post-swarm debris cleanup molecule","description":"After a 20+ worker swarm completed, found significant beads debris:
- 18 stale messages (work assignments, lifecycle requests, swarm instructions)
- 3 completed issues still open/in_progress
- Test messages accumulated

Need: Document a post-swarm checklist or create gt swarm cleanup command that:
1. Closes stale work assignment messages
2. Reviews in_progress issues for completion
3. Closes orphaned lifecycle messages
4. Optionally archives test messages","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"chore","created_at":"2025-12-20T03:12:28.646175-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T03:15:45.521085-08:00"}
